content.text: Let me examine the generated Nomad job file to see what's wrong with the artifact configuration:

input.description: Check artifact configuration in generated job file

content.content:
# - Binary distribution via SeaweedFS artifact download

job "seaweedfs-filer" {
  datacenters = ["dc1"]
  type = "service"
  priority = 75

  # Single instance for development, can be scaled in production
  group "filer" {
    count = 1

--
      artifact {
        source = "https://github.com/seaweedfs/seaweedfs/releases/download/3.96/linux_amd64.tar.gz"
        destination = "local/"
        mode = "any"
      }

      # Environment variables for filer configuration
      env {
        SEAWEEDFS_MASTER = "localhost:9333"
        SEAWEEDFS_DATA_CENTER = "dc1"
        SEAWEEDFS_RACK = "rack1"

content.thinking: The artifact configuration looks correct. It should download to `local/linux_amd64.tar.gz`. But the issue might be that when we change directory with `cd local`, we're looking for the file in the wrong place. Let me fix the script to not change directory and use the full path:

content.text: The artifact configuration looks correct - it downloads to `local/linux_amd64.tar.gz`. The issue is that when we `cd local`, we're looking for the file in the current directory, but it should be looking in the original location. Let me fix the script:

chmod +x weed

chmod +x local/weed
